feedback.text: I would like to see an addition including a way to protect consumers from losing video games they have already paid for. This is also a separate initiative named "Stop Killing Games" that has received way over 1 million signatures as of writing. Imagine how surprised I was when I went to boot up "The Crew" after not playing for a while. A game I though was single player with mere basic online functionality to fill the world and not make it look so empty. Never once had I interacted with another person in that game. But when I tried to play it, I couldn't. The game I paid for was no longer able to be played by me for reasons I couldn't understand. Companies needs to be held accountable to either make their sales more clear and not be able to brick a game I bought for no other reason than to buy the next one, or make them plan their games to be kept with the community after shutting down their own servers. Preferably both options.
